note: Query this registry before creating a new component (anti-bloat rule). All backwards are hand-derived and FD-verified; there is no autograd in this crate.

components.name: conv2d
components.purpose: Learned 2-D convolution — the Neocognitron S-cell (spatial feature detector).
components.output_schema: y: (c_out, H', W') flat f32; grads (grad_x, ConvLayer)
known_limitations: naive (no im2col); fine at G<=32, revisit for large maps

components.name: group_pool
components.purpose: Dihedral group-orbit attention pool — the rotation-equivariant Neocognitron C-cell (invariant resp + equivariant orient).
known_limitations: gives LOCAL orientation-invariance, NOT global rotation-invariance (see F-N3-2)
discrete C_n; between-group angles only approx

components.name: sc_block
components.purpose: Stackable S/C block: conv S-cell (C_in->2K) -> bank of K oriented-unit group_pool C-cells -> K-channel invariant resp map. Output shape = next block input, so blocks stack.
components.output_schema: resp: (K, H', W') invariant; composed backward FD-verified through both cells
known_limitations: C-cell is LOAD-BEARING only in compositional / can't-fit regimes (F-N3-1); REDUNDANT under a learnable single block (F-N2b-1)

components.name: oriented_sobel_bank / ScBlock::new_oriented
components.purpose: Oriented Sobel S-cell warm-start (rotated gradient pair per unit). Canonical cold-start fix for a block feeding an entropy pool.
supported_tasks: any ScBlock (c_in=1, 3x3) that stalls from a random cold start
known_limitations: a prior, not a guarantee — conv stays learnable

components.name: global_entropy_pool
components.purpose: Globally rotation-invariant top: per-channel response-weighted spatial covariance -> [mean, trace, eigen-entropy Hs] (invariant, arrangement-sensitive) + principal-axis angle (equivariant pose readout).
known_limitations: needs an ORIENTED S-cell upstream (cold-start guard F-ENT-2)
validated on controlled 2-shape task; not scaled to clutter
components.note: Recommended top for the rotation-invariant recognition path (mean-top is arrangement-blind, held-out AUROC ~chance).

components.name: soft_argmax
components.purpose: Differentiable keypoint head (integral pose regression / DSNT).

components.name: auroc
components.purpose: Area under ROC (Mann-Whitney U) — canonical binary-eval utility.
components.supersedes: duplicated `fn auroc` in neocognitron_deep/entropy/aniso (removed); remaining copies in cpml_signed_link/signed_link/cr_edge_encoder flagged for consolidation

components.name: skeleton_hg_refine (NOT YET EXTRACTED)
components.purpose: Residual signed hg_conv refinement of predicted joint coords over a skeleton graph (cycles/signs/scale + shared elin).
supported_tasks: structural coord refinement where recovery is a single-neighbour translation (P3: 2.6 vs 6.9px)
components.observation_schema: coord: (J,2) + skeleton (cycles, signs, scale) + LinearLayer elin
known_limitations: shared elin expresses neighbour-average / single-neighbour-translation ONLY; CANNOT express asymmetric affine loop closure (P4 HURTS: 10.7 vs 7.3px, F-P4-1)
components.extraction_blocked_reason: Not yet canonical: P4 measured that the shared-elin form is wrong for closed loops. The path to CANONICAL is the PER-EDGE TRANSFORM op (each hyperedge owns its map). Extract to a `SkeletonRefine` framework component ONCE the per-edge transform lands, so the canonical version is the correct one, not the limited one.

components.name: EvolventHead
components.purpose: Incremental/online (evolvent) MULTI-OUTPUT readout — exact forgetting recursive-least-squares over a fixed feature basis; C=1 regression, C=n_classes one-hot -> argmax classification (precision P SHARED across outputs, step O(d^2 + d*C)); per-sample closed-form, NO backward sweep; tracks drift via lambda; covariance-windup guard on by default.
components.source_experiment: evolvent E0 (streaming) + E1 (static benchmark, 50 seeds, kato15)
supported_tasks: static supervised regression AND classification (one-pass, no tuning)
components.observation_schema: phi: &[f32] (d features), y: &[f32] (C targets; one-hot for classification)
components.output_schema: predict -> Vec<f32> (C); predict_class -> argmax; update -> per-output residuals; verified vs batch ridge
known_limitations: O(d^2) per update (win is SAMPLE efficiency / one-pass / no-tuning, not per-step FLOPs)
long-drift STREAMING is windup-limited (F-EVO-3); needs directional forgetting
linear-in-features (readout only); MLP still edges it where learned features matter (iris, california)
components.note: DEPLOYABLE for STATIC supervised readouts (F-EVO-4, 50 seeds fixed-RNG: COMPETITIVE with 200-epoch MLP -- wins 2 loses 2 ties 2 -- beats SGD 6/6, 1-pass no-tuning). Streaming/drift still EXPERIMENTAL (F-EVO-3).

components.name: BlockEvolventHead
components.purpose: Block-structured (hyperedge-clique) precision evolvent -- the alternative to the dense O(d^2) PAIRWISE precision. Contiguous feature blocks (one per hyperedge), block-diagonal precision (small b_e x b_e per block) with a SHARED residual/denominator; step is O(sum b_e^2) = O(d*w). Keeps higher-order interactions (the block features can be k-way) at a fraction of the cost.
components.observation_schema: phi: &[f32] grouped into contiguous blocks (block_sizes), y: f32
components.output_schema: predict -> f32; update -> prequential residual; single block == dense EvolventHead exactly
known_limitations: drops cross-block (separator) coupling -> approximate for non-additive cross-hyperedge structure
contiguous blocks only (assumes features grouped by hyperedge)
the higher-order term must be an explicit feature
components.note: F-EVO-5: matches dense-hyperedge R2 0.997 at 20x less precision storage; pairwise precision provably cannot represent the 3-way term. Path to CANONICAL: junction-tree (block-tridiagonal) form + update through hg_message + SBSH width certificate.
